business_ideas.idea: Haunted house pop-up - rent vacant property for month, charge $40/entry
Baby businesses within larger companies - acquisition and operator arbitrage

predictions.text: Elon will continue to operate Twitter via unconventional guerrilla tactics, like walking in with a sink
Many companies have undervalued internal businesses that can be spun out profitably

dynamics: Sam drives comedic reaction to Twitter news; Shaan provides analytical foil. Both animated by 'Ligma' troll story—elaborate prank featuring fake tech employee personas with joke names. High entertainment value with underlying business model discovery.
frameworks: Haunted house economics—capital-light operation using temporary retail arbitrage
Baby businesses inside larger companies—misaligned incentives create acquisition opportunities
learnings: Haunted houses generate $500k-$1M revenue in single month via pop-up retail
Elon's Twitter takeover displayed pure conviction-based execution with minimal formal process
Internet trolls can create viral stories indistinguishable from real employee departures
Working on least profitable but most interesting projects reveals true motivation
Humans need purposeful work—extended retirement without contribution reduces lifespan perception

banger_quotes.quote: Elon just walked in—playground rules. That's all it took to own Twitter
Derrick Lewis doesn't do jiu-jitsu, he just gets up
I want to work on the most creative project, even if it makes zero dollars

numbers_dropped: Haunted house: $40 entry fee, estimated $500k-$1M monthly revenue
Podcast initial plan: -$10k/year loss, now profitable plus most fulfilling project
Property rental option: pay property owner $10-15k to use vacant space
notable: Episode showcases MFM brand—finding legitimate business model (haunted houses) alongside entertaining cultural moment (Twitter employee pranks); illustrates how leisure/entertainment and business often intersect in discovery
